summary: IDEA / LOW PRIORITY - research only. ComfyUI v0.28.0 lists 'Support PID 1.5 models' (#14894), a newer generation of the PixelDiT upscaler we already ship as nvidia-pid (MPI-182: one model, four VAE-locked 1024->4096 4-step bf16 checkpoints at 2.72GB each, selected at runtime via Input_Type; compat is the VAE latent space, not the model name). Answer before any wiring: what changed in 1.5 (quality/steps/speed/VRAM), whether the four-checkpoint structure holds (drop-in weight swap vs workflow + Input_Type change), whether it needs ComfyUI >=0.28 (i.e. gated behind MPI-342), and REPLACE vs ADD - replacing means re-uploading ~2.7GB per leg to R2 and every existing user re-downloading, so decide on a measured quality/wall-clock delta, not novelty. Note nvidia-pid is a requiredModel of the sdxl-4k test app that MPI-332 rips - the app's fate is not the model's. Full detail in brief.md.
